critic_raw:
Heavy left-padding for short visit histories requires masking — without it, the LSTM processes substantial uninformative signal before the actual clinical history begins. Masking is not specified.

The logistic regression receives CCI directly while the RNN must reconstruct comorbidity from sequential diagnoses — a feature encoding asymmetry that may advantage the logistic regression for comorbidity-driven readmissions.

Single-visit patients receive entirely zero-padded RNN inputs, reducing effective sample size for evaluating sequential modeling benefits; the proportion of such patients is not reported.
all_issues_raised: Heavy left-padding with zeros for short visit histories means the LSTM processes uninformative signal before clinical content; masking of padded positions is not specified, potentially degrading RNN efficiency for patients with few prior visits.

The logistic regression receives CCI as a direct feature while the RNN must reconstruct comorbidity from sequential diagnoses, creating a feature encoding asymmetry that may advantage the logistic regression for comorbidity-driven readmissions.

Single-visit patients with no prior history receive entirely zero-padded RNN inputs, reducing the effective sample size for evaluating sequential modeling benefits; the proportion of such patients is not reported.

round_transcripts.critic:
Heavy left-padding for short visit histories requires masking — without it, the LSTM processes substantial uninformative signal before the actual clinical history begins. Masking is not specified.

The logistic regression receives CCI directly while the RNN must reconstruct comorbidity from sequential diagnoses — a feature encoding asymmetry that may advantage the logistic regression for comorbidity-driven readmissions.

Single-visit patients receive entirely zero-padded RNN inputs, reducing effective sample size for evaluating sequential modeling benefits; the proportion of such patients is not reported.
round_transcripts.defender:
Left-padding with zeros and masking is standard in sequence model implementation — any competent LSTM implementation includes padding masks to prevent zero-padded positions from contributing to the gradient update or loss. This is a standard implementation detail that does not need to be specified at the experimental design level.

CCI is included in the logistic regression as a summary feature while the RNN receives the underlying diagnosis sequences that generate CCI scores. The RNN has access to more granular information (specific diagnoses, their temporal pattern) than the CCI summary. If the RNN can learn temporal diagnosis patterns, it has an informational advantage over CCI — the logistic regression’s CCI feature is a compressed version of what the RNN has in raw form. This is not an asymmetry favoring logistic regression.

Single-visit patients are included in both model evaluations; the RNN falls back to static features for these patients, as does the logistic regression. The comparison is fair for single-visit patients: both models predict without sequence history. The proportion of single-visit patients affects the average RNN advantage but does not bias the comparison. Reporting this proportion is a descriptive analysis enhancement, not a design requirement.
